svg.title: Resumable upload system design
svg.desc: Full architecture diagram showing chunked upload flow, server components, storage layers, and crash recovery paths

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How should I implement file chunking in the browser with JavaScript?')
sendPrompt('What should I store in client-side localStorage for upload resumability?')
g.text: uploadId · fileHash (SHA-256) · chunkSize · uploadedChunks[ ] · fileSize · filename

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me the POST /uploads/init endpoint implementation')
sendPrompt('Show me the PUT /uploads/:id/chunk/:n endpoint implementation')
sendPrompt('Show me the GET /uploads/:id/status endpoint implementation')
sendPrompt('How does idempotency work for chunk uploads?')
g.text: All endpoints are idempotent — re-uploading chunk N is always safe

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What should the upload session record in the database contain?')
sendPrompt('How do I verify chunk integrity with MD5 checksums?')
sendPrompt('How should I implement the missing chunks query?')
sendPrompt('What is the database schema for tracking upload chunk state?')
sendPrompt('How do I write individual chunks to S3 for later assembly?')
sendPrompt('What role does Redis play in a resumable upload system?')
sendPrompt('How do I trigger file assembly when all chunks are uploaded?')
g.text: Stream-concatenate .part files → verify full-file SHA-256 → move to final path → delete .part files

svg.text: ⑥ RETRY FLOW (connection drop / server crash)

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does the client detect that a connection was lost during upload?')
sendPrompt('How does exponential backoff work for retrying chunk uploads?')
sendPrompt('Show me the GET /uploads/:id/status response format')
sendPrompt('How does the client know which chunks to skip on retry?')
sendPrompt('How do I make chunk writes atomic so a server crash mid-write is safe?')
sendPrompt('How do I detect and recover orphaned partial chunk writes after a crash?')
sendPrompt('How do I implement upload session TTL and abandoned upload cleanup?')
sendPrompt('What is the optimal chunk size for large file uploads?')
sendPrompt('Should I use parallel or sequential chunk uploads? What are the tradeoffs?')
sendPrompt('How should I implement chunk integrity verification with checksums?')
sendPrompt('Tell me about the tus.io resumable upload protocol')
sendPrompt('How does S3 Multipart Upload work and when should I use it?')
sendPrompt('What is the Google Resumable Upload API and how does it work?')

svg.text: Click any node to go deeper ↗
The invariant: a chunk written to object store is permanent. DB is the ledger. On any crash, the gap between the two is the only work to redo.
The client never retransmits what the server already durably holds.
The server never has to reconstruct state it already committed.
